beats.activity: Wake up and get ready for the day
beats.reasoning: Henry's routine: early riser who can't start without a swim. Low energy yesterday (35/100) means he'll need the water reset even more today. Wakes before pool opens.
Henry's non-negotiable morning ritual—cold water clears his head, especially after low-energy days. Pool opens early; he'll be there for the quiet morning session.

beats.activity: Commute to work, transit strike forces alternative route
beats.reasoning: Metro buses and Link suspended until 4pm; Henry will drive or rideshare. Rideshare surge pricing is active but he needs to get to work. Budgets 45 min for the strike chaos and potential traffic.

beats.activity: Arrive at school and prep for the day; Back-to-back counseling sessions and check-ins
beats.reasoning: School counselor settling in before morning sessions. Uses the buffer to review notes on kids like Marcus, who've had tough week.

beats.activity: Lunch and oat milk latte at familiar café
beats.reasoning: Henry's go-to spot for his ritual oat milk latte and decompress time. Frugal and creature-of-habit; same place every day resets his mind.

beats.activity: Afternoon counseling sessions, paperwork, and school duties
beats.reasoning: Afternoon block at school. Henry stays present with students through end of day, then handles admin work. Work ends as transit strike lifts at 4pm.

beats.activity: Commute home via transit (strike ended at 4pm)
beats.reasoning: Transit restored at 4pm; Henry takes Link Light Rail back to Beacon Hill. Easy 50-min ride gives him time to decompress after work.

beats.activity: Community garden tending: water beds, check tomato plants
beats.reasoning: Partly cloudy and 68F—perfect garden weather. Henry values getting his hands in soil to ground himself after emotional work. His tomatoes are thriving; this is restorative.
Thursday night salsa with Miguel and friends—Henry's joy after heavy work days. Stays in Beacon Hill, no long commute needed. Values close friends over crowds.

diary: Started the day with my usual swim at Beacon Hill Pool—the water was perfect, and I needed it after a restless night. The transit strike threw me off my rhythm though. Had to take a rideshare to First Hill Elementary, which felt wasteful, but I made it in time for the morning rush of kids needing to talk. One second-grader opened up about anxiety around his parents' divorce; those moments remind me why I'm here. Grabbed my oat milk latte at Café Solstice around lunch and just sat for a minute, watching the city hum outside. Afternoon was heavy—more sessions, more paperwork—but I powered through. By the time the buses came back online at 4pm, I was ready to decompress. Watered the tomato beds at the garden, and they're finally looking robust. Ended the night dancing salsa with friends, which felt like exactly what my body needed. Tired but settled.
thought_process: The strike disrupted my usual flow this morning, and I'll admit I was irritated about surge pricing—felt like a waste when I usually bike or take the bus. But once I got to school, that frustration melted into focus. I had a particularly vulnerable kid this morning, and it recalibrated my priorities. The afternoon felt long, so I made sure to take my lunch break properly instead of eating at my desk like I usually do. The garden and salsa dancing were intentional choices to shake off the heaviness of the day. I think I needed to move my body and be around people I trust. The weather helped too—bright enough to lift the mood without being oppressive. By evening, I felt more like myself.
world_event_prompt: Mild, partly cloudy Thursday, high of 68F. Citywide transit strike: all Metro buses and Link Light Rail suspended until 4pm. Rideshare surge pricing in effect.
